pattern: 2D dynamic programming where dp[i][j] is the maximum dot product using the first i elements of nums1 and first j elements of nums2.
hints: Define dp[i][j] as the maximum dot product achievable from non-empty subsequences of nums1[0..i-1] and nums2[0..j-1].
Transition: dp[i][j] = max(nums1[i-1]*nums2[j-1], dp[i-1][j-1] + nums1[i-1]*nums2[j-1], dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).
Be careful when extending the subsequence: only add a positive previous product; using max(0, dp[i-1][j-1]) handles the case where it is better to pick just the current pair.

edge_inputs.description: both arrays length 1, negative product forced
all negatives — must pick one pair
best is single element pick from far ends
extending is better than single pair
all values negative — best dot product is still negative

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    m = rng.randint(1, size)
    nums1 = [rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    nums2 = [rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(m)]
    return [nums1, nums2]
